title: daucus gingidium subsp. rupestris
intro: Daucus gingidium subsp. rupestris, also known as mountain carrot, is a subspecies of plant within the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of furanocoumarins and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as supporting digestive health, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Daucus gingidium subsp. rupestris is often prepared as an herbal tea or used in infusions, and it can also be found in some herbal supplements and tinctures.
